channel.title: Raffles Xingqi Zhu
channel.description: Recent content on Raffles Xingqi Zhu

item.title: Paper Published in Vision Research: Amblyopic Acuity and Scrambled Letters
item.description: Our full study on amblyopic letter identification with spatially scrambled stimuli is now published in Vision Research.

item.title: BioRxiv Update: Spatial Scrambling and Letter Identification
item.description: Version 2 of our BioRxiv preprint on physiologically-inspired spatial scrambling and letter identification is now online.

item.title: BioRxiv Preprint: Amblyopic Acuity and Spatial Scrambling
item.description: Preprint of our full study on amblyopic letter identification efficiency with scrambled stimuli, now on BioRxiv.

item.title: My JOV Abstract Is Now Online
item.description: My Journal of Vision abstract on spatial scrambling and amblyopic letter acuity is now online.

item.title: New arXiv Paper: Stereo Geometry Errors in HMDs
item.description: Our new arXiv paper studies how stereo geometry errors in HMDs affect perceived distance and visuomotor behavior.

item.title: My JOV 2024 Abstract Is Now Online
item.description: My 2024 Journal of Vision abstract on spatial scrambling and letter identification is now online.

item.title: Started my internship at Meta Reality Labs
item.description: I just started an internship on the Display System Research team at Meta Reality Labs.

item.title: ACS Nano Paper: Single-Particle Measurements of Drug-Delivery Nanoparticles
item.description: Our ACS Nano paper on single-particle measurements of nanoparticle size and loading is online.

Hi, I’m currently working towards my PhD under the co-supervision of Alex Baldwin and Robert Hess at McGill University in Montreal, Canada. I am a former reseasrch intern at Meta Reality Labs doing applied vision science for head-mounted displays. When I’m not in the lab, I like taking photos for fun. You can check out my photography portofio here.

2024

Spatial scrambling in human vision: investigating efficiency for discriminating scrambled letters using convolutional neural networks and confusion matrices

VSS 2024

Identification of letters distorted by physiologically-inspired spatial scrambling

BioRxiv

2021

Simultaneous, Single-Particle Measurements of Size and Loading Give Insights into the Structure of Drug-Delivery Nanoparticles

ACS Nano
